reasoning: As of June 5, 2025, the seven countries listed (China, Iran, Myanmar, North Korea, Russia, Turkmenistan, and Venezuela) all maintain strict censorship regimes that include long-standing or consolidated blocks on X (formerly Twitter). China, Iran, North Korea, and Turkmenistan have blocked the platform for many years. Myanmar and Russia implemented blocks following the 2021 coup and 2022 invasion of Ukraine, respectively, with no signs of restoration. Venezuela, the most recent addition, blocked X in August 2024; although initially termed a '10-day' ban, evidence from late 2024 and early 2025 indicates the block persisted as part of Maduro's crackdown on dissent following disputed elections. Wikipedia's 'Censorship of Twitter' article consistently reflects these blocks in its 'Current' section. Given the short horizon until July 1, 2025 (less than one month), and the stable authoritarian nature of the censoring regimes, it is highly probable that all seven will remain listed. The 'all or nothing' resolution criteria (any one country being unblocked or removed from the list would trigger a 'No') suggests a slightly more conservative probability than for an individual country, but a 0.88 remains appropriate given the entrenched nature of these blocks.
